beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Felix starts the day at home with full energy. Early wake on a sunny Monday to get ahead of the day.

beats.activity: Commute to yoga studio in Redmond.
beats.reasoning: Drive from Kirkland to Redmond workplace; 35 min allows for typical Monday morning traffic on I-90.

beats.activity: Lead morning yoga and wellness coaching sessions.
beats.reasoning: Two morning classes back-to-back is typical for Felix's schedule; good energy on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Break at studio, hydrate and prep for FIFA match.
beats.reasoning: Felix knows the USA vs Wales match is at 11am; he's deciding whether to catch it downtown or skip it. Sarcastic and social, he'd rather be with people than watch alone.

beats.activity: Drive from Redmond to downtown Seattle sports bar.
beats.reasoning: Felix heads downtown to catch the 11am match with friends at a bar near Pike Place; 40 min drive accounts for light traffic and parking.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA match with friends, grab lunch and drinks.
beats.reasoning: Felix is generous with money and values close friends; a sunny Monday and early-opening bar make this an easy social call. The match runs 11am–1pm, plus pre/post-game chat.

beats.activity: Wander Pike Place and hit up a thrift store.
beats.reasoning: Felix loves thrifting and knows the area well; lingering downtown on a sunny day is in character. Light Rail closure (9am–3pm Westlake–U-District) doesn't affect him here.

beats.activity: Commute back to Redmond for afternoon coaching session.
beats.reasoning: 40 min drive back to work; Light Rail closure is irrelevant since Felix drives.

beats.activity: Teach afternoon yoga classes and wellness consultations.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon block of teaching; Felix maintains consistent income and client relationships.

beats.activity: Stop at food truck (Taco Tuesday special) on way home.
beats.reasoning: Felix knows every food truck's rotation and plans around it; grabbing dinner before heading home keeps evening relaxed.
35 min evening commute from Redmond to Kirkland; light traffic after 5pm.

diary: Woke up early with that Monday energy—coffee hit different today. Classes at the studio were solid; people were actually present, you know? Around mid-morning I felt the pull of the World Cup fever everyone was talking about, so I made an executive decision to bail early and head downtown. Pike Place was packed with folks streaming into bars, and catching the USA-Wales match with friends was exactly the reset I needed. The energy was infectious, even though the game itself was pretty tense. Grabbed some lunch, wandered through the thrift aisles (found a vintage Patagonia fleece, score), then drove back to Redmond for the afternoon grind. Taught a couple of evening classes, then hit up that taco truck on the way home—those al pastor tacos were calling my name. Home by seven, completely wiped but satisfied. Days like this remind me why I love this city.
thought_process: I almost stuck to my usual routine, but the World Cup thing got in my head—and honestly, I needed the break. Teaching morning classes was fulfilling but routine, and I realized I was craving community energy beyond the studio. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but it made me aware of how the city was reshaping itself around maintenance. Skipping the afternoon commute stress by going straight to Pike Place felt spontaneous and right. By the time I got back to Redmond, I was recharged enough to teach with genuine enthusiasm rather than autopilot. Small decisions like stopping at the taco truck instead of cooking felt earned. The day had natural rhythm—work, play, work, rest—and I leaned into it rather than fighting it.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
